data.title: MDU Resources to acquire 49% ownership interest in Badger Wind Farm
data.description:
• MDU Resources Group on Friday said its subsidiary, Montana-Dakota Utilities (NYSE:MDU), has signed a purchase agreement to acquire a 49% ownership interest in the Badger Wind Farm for $294 million.
  • The acquisition represents 122.5 MW of the project's total 250 MW generation capacity.
  • MDU previously executed a power purchase agreement (PPA) for 150 MW of the Badger Wind Farm's output last November, which included the 49% purchase option MDU has now exercised under the purchase agreement. With this ownership purchase, the capacity under the PPA will be reduced to 27.5 MW upon closing.
  • MDU +0.39% after hours to $16.8368.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CoStar Group acquires Ag-Analytics
data.description:
• CoStar Group(NASDAQ:CSGP) on Friday said it has acquired agricultural data company Ag-Analytics.
  • The company said the acquisition will strengthen the data capabilities for farmland, forestland, recreational, and other rural properties through Ag-Analytics’ AcreValue platform, providing innovative outlet for farmers, land investors, land professionals and landowners to gather the most accurate insights and data on agricultural land.
  • Land.com, CoStar Group’s marketplace for discovering, buying, and selling rural real estate, will incorporate AcreValue’s reports and analytics into all applicable listings.
  • CoStar said these reports include geographical data, soil composition, elevations, historical and other information. With 1.5 million registered users on AcreValue and data on tens of millions of parcels, this integration will provide insights for clients.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: President Trump to announce auto tariffs in April
data.description:
As part of his broader trade policy to bring manufacturing back to the U.S. and level the playing field with trade partners, President Trump said on Friday he would announce tariffs on automobiles imported into the U.S. on April 2 giving auto executives seven weeks to change the president’s mind or make alternate manufacturing plans.

“There’s a global street fight in the auto industry right now,” Ford CEO Jim Farley said at an investor event early this week, cautioning participants that tariffs will create a “lot of cost and a lot of chaos.”

The tariffs would predominately target non-U.S. auto manufacturers like Volkswagen (VWAGY, VWAPY), Hyundai-Kia (OTCPK:HYMTF), Mercedes-Benz (MBGAF, MBGYY), and BMW (OTCPK:BMWKY), among others. But will also put a dent in manufacturing for Detroit’s Big 3, all of which offshore much of their production to capitalize on cheaper labor. General Motors' (NYSE:GM) Buick division makes most of its cars in South Korea, and many Chevy models like the Blazer and Silverado in Mexico. Ford’s (F) Bronco Sport, Maverick, and Mustang Mach-E are all manufactured in Mexico, while the Edge SUV comes from Canada.

The new tariffs are exclusive of tariffs currently in place on imported vehicles from China.

The Trump Administration’s trade policy aims to address the significant imbalance between imported and exported vehicles in and out of the U.S. At nearly $240B, automobiles are the most imported commodity into the U.S., making it the world’s largest auto importer. As a result, half of all cars on U.S. roads come from outside the country, with Mexico and Canada in the top spot, followed by Japan, South Korea, and Germany. And while the U.S. pays a 10% tax on cars from the EU, U.S. cars imported in the EU are hit with a 10% tariff.

“We all love Europe, love the countries in Europe, but the European Union has been absolutely brutal on trade," Trump said, adding "The EU's been very nasty, and they haven't treated us properly."

It’s uncertain what are the specific targets of new tariffs, and how it will impact vehicles built in Mexico and Canada that come under the USMCA agreement of 2020. Under terms of the USMCA, vehicles manufactured in either Mexico or Canada are exempt from import taxes as long as at least 75% of the vehicle’s components are made in either the U.S., Canada or Mexico, 70% of the vehicle’s aluminum must be made in North America, and at least 70% of a vehicle’s steel must be domestically melted and poured. In addition, at least 40% to 50% of a vehicle’s value must be sourced from facilities where workers make at least $16 per hour.

Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Moderna remains the most shorted stock among S&P 500 healthcare stocks in the first month of 2025
data.description:
Short interest for healthcare stocks in S&P 500 rose slightly in January vs. December, while Moderna continued to be the most shorted stock of the month.

The average short interest for S&P 500 Health Care Index Sector (NYSEARCA:XLV) came at 1.60%, higher than 1.58% recorded last month.

XLV, which has a 12.12% weightage in the broader S&P 500 index, rose 4.6% in the last month, compared to S&P 500 index's (SP500) rise of 4.9%.

Industry analysis:

Average short Interest as percentage of floating shares

Biotechnology was the most shorted industry in the healthcare index in January for the tenth time in a row. Short interest in this sub-sector rose to 2.45% from 2.34% in December.

Healthcare provider and services sector was in the second position with short interest at 1.90% in January end.

Pharmaceuticals had the lowest short interest in the month of January, with a short percentage of 1.28%.

Stocks with highest and lowest short positions

Ranked by short interest as a percentage of shares float

Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA)—most shorted—saw short interest rose to 11.84% from 11.03% in December. DaVita (NYSE:DVA) came in second with a short interest of 5.81%, followed by ResMed (NYSE:RMD) with a short interest of 5.65%.

Boston Scientific (NYSE:BSX) was the least shorted stock in January, with a short interest of 0.71%, followed by Danaher Corp (NYSE:DHR) and Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ), both of which came in a close second with a short percentage of 0.78%.
